message: test(cli): harden cost command error paths

files.patch: @@ -1,7 +1,7 @@ {"id":"bd-11kb","title":"Add missing cache headers to user-data API routes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T07:03:31.731604860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T07:33:34.099566550Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:33:34.099272495Z","close_reason":"Added cache headers to 8 API routes: featured (public), history (private), ratings (conditional), share/mine (private), referral/stats (private), referral/code (private), share/[code] (public), status/incidents (public)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-1d6d","title":"Remove ultrathink instruction from prompt text","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T02:22:53.539441606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T02:30:42.663176238Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T02:30:42.662998253Z","close_reason":"Removed ultrathink instructions from prompt content and exports","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-1eho","title":"Feature: Auto-Tagging + Duplicate Detection (Metadata Assistant)","description":"## Background As the prompt library grows, manual tagging becomes inconsistent and duplicates sneak in. Pro users need fast, reliable metadata and dedupe support. ## Scope - Auto-suggest tags, categories, and short descriptions using deterministic rules + semantic similarity (no SKILL.md). - Duplicate/near-duplicate detection with similarity thresholds and preview diffs. - Merge flow: mark as alias, merge tags, or ignore with rationale. - CLI: `jfp tags suggest ` and `jfp dedupe scan` (Pro-only). ## Deliverables - Metadata suggestion service + similarity index. - UI panel for suggestions and duplicate resolution. - CLI scan output with JSON option. - Admin override for global tag mappings. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Similarity scoring thresholds and tag suggestion rules. - Duplicate detection determinism. ### Integration Tests - Suggestions saved to prompt metadata and reflected in search. - Dedupe merge updates references (packs/workflows/collections). ### E2E Tests - Create prompt -> receive suggestions -> accept -> verify tags. - Detect duplicate -> merge -> verify references updated. ## Success Criteria - Tag consistency improves without manual rework. - Duplicate prompts are surfaced and resolvable. - Search quality improves for Pro users.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:48.758255166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:31:01.088192808Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} -{"id":"bd-2auv","title":"Feature: Pro Cost Estimator + Budget Alerts (Web + CLI)","description":"## Background Pro users need predictable costs and time budgeting. They want to see estimated token usage and set monthly caps before running prompts. ## Scope - Deterministic token + cost estimator per prompt + model (web + CLI). - UI badges showing estimated tokens and cost; compare estimate vs actual after runs. - Budget configuration (monthly cap + per-run threshold) with alerts. - CLI: `jfp cost ` and `jfp run --estimate`, plus budget status in `jfp status`. - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Shared pricing table + cost calculation utility. - UI components + settings page for budgets and alerts. - CLI commands and Pro-gated upgrade messaging. - Alert logging/audit trail for over-budget events. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Cost calculation across models and price tiers. - Budget threshold logic and alert formatting. ### Integration Tests - Estimator uses prompt variables and produces stable output. - Budget updates persist and are read by CLI. ### E2E Tests - Set budget -> estimate -> exceed -> alert shown in UI and CLI. ## Success Criteria - Estimates within acceptable tolerance vs actual token usage. - Users can see cost before running prompts. - Budget alerts prevent surprise overages.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:36.190844227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:33.398133847Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":16,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Web UI cost badges implemented in commit fc824eb. CostBadge added to PromptCard and PromptDetailModal. Remaining: budget settings page and alert logging.","created_at":"2026-01-31T06:33:35Z"},{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Session 2026-01-31: Added CostBadge tests (commit 3e83b39). Web badges complete. Remaining work: budget settings page requires backend/database infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T06:46:06Z"}]} +{"id":"bd-2auv","title":"Feature: Pro Cost Estimator + Budget Alerts (Web + CLI)","description":"## Background Pro users need predictable costs and time budgeting. They want to see estimated token usage and set monthly caps before running prompts. ## Scope - Deterministic token + cost estimator per prompt + model (web + CLI). - UI badges showing estimated tokens and cost; compare estimate vs actual after runs. - Budget configuration (monthly cap + per-run threshold) with alerts. - CLI: `jfp cost ` and `jfp run --estimate`, plus budget status in `jfp status`. - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Shared pricing table + cost calculation utility. - UI components + settings page for budgets and alerts. - CLI commands and Pro-gated upgrade messaging. - Alert logging/audit trail for over-budget events. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Cost calculation across models and price tiers. - Budget threshold logic and alert formatting. ### Integration Tests - Estimator uses prompt variables and produces stable output. - Budget updates persist and are read by CLI. ### E2E Tests - Set budget -> estimate -> exceed -> alert shown in UI and CLI. ## Success Criteria - Estimates within acceptable tolerance vs actual token usage. - Users can see cost before running prompts. - Budget alerts prevent surprise overages.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:13:36.190844227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:57:52.753466788Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":16,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Web UI cost badges implemented in commit fc824eb. CostBadge added to PromptCard and PromptDetailModal. Remaining: budget settings page and alert logging.","created_at":"2026-01-31T06:33:35Z"},{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-2auv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Session 2026-01-31: Added CostBadge tests (commit 3e83b39). Web badges complete. Remaining work: budget settings page requires backend/database infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T06:46:06Z"}]} {"id":"bd-2kwr","title":"Fix support ticket rate limiting - uses in-memory Maps that reset on deploy","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-29T07:02:52.563632334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T07:23:01.666524852Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:23:01.666361565Z","close_reason":"Fixed by extracting rate limiting to modular lib/rate-limit module with documentation and Upstash upgrade path","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-2txr","title":"E2E Tests: History UI Components","description":"## Task Add UI-level E2E tests for the Recently Viewed History feature. ## Context The API-level E2E tests exist (e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts) but UI component tests are missing. ## Test Scenarios 1. **RecentlyViewedSidebar Component** - Sidebar displays recent items - Clear history button works - Items link to correct pages - Empty state displays correctly - Loading state shows correctly 2. **History Page (/history)** - Page loads and displays items - Search/filter works - Clear all history works - Timestamps display correctly - Links navigate correctly 3. **History Tracking Integration** - Viewing a prompt adds to history - Search queries are tracked - Deduplication prevents rapid duplicates - Items appear in sidebar after viewing ## Files to Modify - e2e/history/recently-viewed.spec.ts (add UI tests) - e2e/lib/history-helpers.ts (add UI selectors) ## Success Criteria - All sidebar interactions tested - History page fully tested - Integration with tracking verified","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralStar","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:45:40.323914674Z","created_by":"CoralStar","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005730906Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:46.005649042Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-2txr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete: - Added 16 new UI tests (34 total including API tests) - History Page UI tests: header, empty state, items display, search filter, clear button, navigation - RecentlyViewedSidebar tests: title, empty state, history link, items display, 8-item limit - History Tracking Integration tests: cross-page sync, clear sync, search query display - Updated history-helpers.ts with better UI selectors for sidebar and history page - 68 total tests (34 × 2 browser configs: chromium + Mobile Chrome)","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:54:39Z"}]} {"id":"bd-2vsu","title":"Feature: Personalized Recommendations Feed (Pro)","description":"## Background Pro users want to discover high‑value prompts quickly without browsing the entire catalog. A personalized feed drives engagement and retention. ## Scope - "For You" feed based on usage signals (views, runs, saves) + explicit preferences. - Explanations: "Because you used X" or "Trending in your category". - Weekly digest email (opt‑in) with top recommendations. - CLI: `jfp recommend` returns a ranked list with rationale. - Prompts/prompt packs only (no SKILL.md or skills content). ## Deliverables - Ranking pipeline and data model for signals. - Feed UI with filters (timeframe, category). - Digest email template + scheduling job. - CLI command with JSON output. ## Test Requirements ### Unit Tests - Ranking weight calculations and deterministic ordering. - Preference filtering and exclusion rules. ### Integration Tests - Signal ingestion -> feed updates -> UI reflects changes. - Digest job generates consistent results. ### E2E Tests - Toggle preferences -> feed updates -> digest preview. - CLI recommend -> matches feed top items. ## Success Criteria - Users find relevant prompts faster. - Feed is explainable and feels trustworthy. - Opt‑in privacy settings are respected.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:14:02.330248162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:30:55.979101810Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} @@ -147,7 +147,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","title":"Add --json flag support for all new CLI commands","description":"Add --json flag support to all new CLI commands for machine-readable output. Commands to Update: - jfp login (output: { authenticated: true, email, tier }) - jfp logout (output: { logged_out: true }) - jfp whoami (output: { email, tier, user_id }) - jfp collections list (output: { collections: [...] }) - jfp collections show (output: { collection, items: [...] }) - jfp notes (output: { prompt_id, notes: [...] }) - jfp save (output: { saved: true, prompt_id }) - jfp list --mine/--saved (output: { prompts: [...] }) Implementation: - Add --json/-j flag to all relevant commands - When --json is set, output only valid JSON to stdout - Errors should also be JSON: { error: true, message, code } - Progress/status messages go to stderr when --json is enabled - Ensure consistent field naming across all commands ","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:23:15.385023105Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:20:01.044458985Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T02:20:01.044458985Z","close_reason":"All CLI commands now have --json flag support with consistent output format","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9g76","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:19.154441027Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9r7s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cgj5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:08.868781233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9rgl","title":"Redesign 'Clear All Filters' as a proper button","description":"# Task: Redesign Clear Filters Button ## Current Implementation (page.tsx:165-172) ```tsx {hasActiveFilters && ( )} ``` ## Problems 1. Plain text link - low visual prominence 2. Uses indigo (inconsistent with neutral design system) 3. Only visible when filters active (no affordance) 4. No icon - relies entirely on text ## Proposed Design ### Option A: Always-visible ghost button (Recommended) ```tsx ``` ### Option B: Only when active, but as a proper button ```tsx {hasActiveFilters && ( )} ``` ## Implementation Details ### Add filter count calculation ```tsx const filterCount = useMemo(() => { let count = 0; if (filters.query) count++; if (filters.category) count++; count += filters.tags.length; return count; }, [filters]); ``` ### Add accessibility ```tsx aria-label={`Clear all ${filterCount} active filters`} ``` ## Design Rationale - Ghost button maintains visual hierarchy (filters are secondary to results) - Always visible = discoverable (disabled state indicates "nothing to clear") - Icon + text = clear meaning - Count badge = precise feedback - Matches CategoryFilter/TagFilter clear button pattern ## Testing 1. With no filters: button disabled, grayed out 2. With 1 filter: button enabled, shows "(1)" 3. With multiple: button shows count 4. Click clears all and disables button 5. Keyboard accessible (Tab, Enter) ## Files Modified - apps/web/src/app/page.tsx ## Dependencies - Should use the updated neutral color palette (do after color fix) - Matches pattern already used in TagFilter.tsx (Clear button)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:38:18.479565228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T15:06:14.296451909Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T15:06:14.296451909Z","close_reason":"Implementation verified complete: Button component with ghost variant, X icon, filterCount memo, aria-label, and neutral colors. Build passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9rgl","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-ogel","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T05:43:15.125413399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","title":"Feature: GitHub Actions E2E Integration","description":"Add E2E tests to GitHub Actions CI workflow. Configure CLI E2E tests to run on push/PR. Configure Playwright for web E2E with proper browser setup. Upload test artifacts (screenshots, videos, logs) on failure. Optional: multi-browser test matrix for comprehensive coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:43:26.454174609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T00:16:26.265863560Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T00:16:26.265863560Z","close_reason":"CI infrastructure implemented in parent EPIC","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-945","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:53:36.860791733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-jxd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:53:26.008125350Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-nfm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:53:59.405040418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wk9","title":"EPIC: Ratings & Reviews System","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-cdp. ## Overview Ratings and reviews provide quality signals for community content, helping users discover the best prompts, packs, and skills while rewarding creators for quality contributions. ## Rating System Design ### Simple Rating (MVP) - Thumbs up / thumbs down (binary) - Easy, low-friction interaction - Clear positive/negative signal - Calculate "approval rate" percentage ### Extended Rating (V2) - 1-5 star rating - Written review (optional) - Helpful/not helpful on reviews - Response from author ## Key Features ### Rate Content - One-click rating on any ratable content - Rate: prompts, packs, skills - Change rating anytime - See your rating history ### View Ratings - Show rating on content cards - Rating breakdown on detail pages - Sort by rating in lists - Filter by minimum rating ### Reviews (V2) - Write text review with rating - Edit/delete own reviews - Report inappropriate reviews - Author can respond to reviews ### Leaderboards - Top rated prompts this week/month - Top contributors by ratings received - Rising stars (new content with high ratings) ## Anti-Gaming Measures - One rating per user per item - Rate limiting on rapid ratings - Flag suspicious patterns - Weight by user reputation ## Database Schema - ratings (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_value, created_at, updated_at) - reviews (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_id, content, created_at) - review_votes (review_id, user_id, is_helpful) ## UI Components - RatingButton (thumbs up/down) - RatingDisplay (show current rating) - RatingBreakdown (detailed stats) - ReviewList (paginated reviews) - ReviewForm (write review) ## Integration Points - Community prompts (Swap Meet) - Prompt packs - Skills - Collections (V2) ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"SilverPine","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:20:02.532432863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:48:43.416591144Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wk9","title":"EPIC: Ratings & Reviews System","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-cdp. ## Overview Ratings and reviews provide quality signals for community content, helping users discover the best prompts, packs, and skills while rewarding creators for quality contributions. ## Rating System Design ### Simple Rating (MVP) - Thumbs up / thumbs down (binary) - Easy, low-friction interaction - Clear positive/negative signal - Calculate "approval rate" percentage ### Extended Rating (V2) - 1-5 star rating - Written review (optional) - Helpful/not helpful on reviews - Response from author ## Key Features ### Rate Content - One-click rating on any ratable content - Rate: prompts, packs, skills - Change rating anytime - See your rating history ### View Ratings - Show rating on content cards - Rating breakdown on detail pages - Sort by rating in lists - Filter by minimum rating ### Reviews (V2) - Write text review with rating - Edit/delete own reviews - Report inappropriate reviews - Author can respond to reviews ### Leaderboards - Top rated prompts this week/month - Top contributors by ratings received - Rising stars (new content with high ratings) ## Anti-Gaming Measures - One rating per user per item - Rate limiting on rapid ratings - Flag suspicious patterns - Weight by user reputation ## Database Schema - ratings (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_value, created_at, updated_at) - reviews (id, user_id, content_type, content_id, rating_id, content, created_at) - review_votes (review_id, user_id, is_helpful) ## UI Components - RatingButton (thumbs up/down) - RatingDisplay (show current rating) - RatingBreakdown (detailed stats) - ReviewList (paginated reviews) - ReviewForm (write review) ## Integration Points - Community prompts (Swap Meet) - Prompt packs - Skills - Collections (V2) ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"SilverPine","created_at":"2026-01-11T06:20:02.532432863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:58:13.022583752Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9wpw","title":"Fix useLocalStorage null persistence","description":"useLocalStorage skipped persisting null values because null used as sentinel; track latest value with a separate flag","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:55:53.211070932Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:56:21.167849624Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T22:56:21.167849624Z","close_reason":"Track latest value with flag so null can persist safely","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9x9","title":"Implement jfp open, categories, tags, doctor, about -- Details: open launches browser to permalink. categories/tags list with counts. doctor checks environment (cache, skills dirs). about shows ecosystem info + ASCII banner.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:27:23.461876267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T07:37:37.501389089Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T07:37:37.501389089Z","close_reason":"Commands already implemented: open, categories, tags, doctor, about. All support --json output.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["status:in_progress"]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9x9.1","title":"State change: status → in_progress","description":"Set status to in_progress","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"event","created_at":"2026-01-10T07:36:05.003876937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T02:23:37.411945766Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T02:23:37.411945766Z","close_reason":"Acknowledged state change - parent bead completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9x9.1","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-10T07:36:05.005026843Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
